profile.title: The Initiated Reader
profile.summary: Your instincts are already moving beyond surface symbolism. You understand that position shapes meaning, that cards in combination create narrative, and that the useful question is rarely what a card "means" in isolation.
profile.startPoint: Begin directly with spread practice. The Minor Arcana will be your focus — you don't need the Major Arcana foundations.

spread.name: Mind · Body · Spirit
spread.description: A snapshot across three dimensions of being.

card.name: The Fool
card.upright: A new chapter is opening. The Fool carries no baggage — literally. He steps off the cliff not from ignorance but from trust. In a reading, this card asks whether you are willing to begin without knowing how it ends. It speaks to pure potential, unformed energy, the courage of not yet knowing.
card.reversed: The leap is being blocked — by fear, by overthinking, or by a reckless jump taken too soon. Reversed, The Fool asks: what are you refusing to begin, or what have you begun without any thought at all?
card.relational: Next to The World: a full cycle ending and beginning. Next to The Tower: the leap may cost more than expected. Next to The High Priestess: trust your instincts before you act.

scenario.love: Beginning something new — a relationship, a feeling, a risk taken in vulnerability.
scenario.work: A career pivot, a new venture, or a moment when jumping in is the only way forward.
scenario.self: The invitation to stop rehearsing and start living.

card.name: The Magician
card.upright: All four suits appear on The Magician's table — cups, wands, swords, pentacles. He has everything he needs. One hand points up, one down: as above, so below. This is the card of directed will — of taking what you have and making something real from it. It asks: what are you capable of that you haven't yet attempted?
card.reversed: Skill used deceptively, or potential squandered. Reversed, The Magician often points to manipulation — someone using their abilities to mislead — or to the querent holding themselves back from their own power.
card.relational: Next to The High Priestess: knowledge and intuition working together, or in tension. Next to The Devil: skill turned to manipulation. Next to The Star: vision finally becoming action.

scenario.love: Someone showing their true capability — or concealing it. The early stage where impressions are everything.
scenario.work: A project where your full skill set is needed. Trust that you have what it takes.
scenario.self: The reminder that capability without application is just potential.

card.name: The Death
card.upright: Death rarely means death. In nearly every context it signals an ending that makes a beginning possible — the kind of change that is so complete there is no going back. Something has run its course. The grief is real, but so is what comes after. In a reading, Death asks what you are clinging to that has already ended, and what would become possible if you let it go.
card.reversed: Resistance to necessary change, clinging to what is already over. Or stagnation so complete that even ending has stalled.
card.relational: Next to The Fool: profound transformation leading to a new beginning. Next to The Tower: sudden, devastating change. Next to The Ten of Cups: the end of a chapter but not of love.

scenario.love: A relationship that has fundamentally changed, or needs to end for both people to grow.
scenario.work: The end of a role, a business phase, or an identity tied to work.
scenario.self: What in your sense of self needs to be allowed to die so something real can replace it?
